park_reason: Live mutable source — re-extraction cannot reproduce frozen gold.
detail: Autopoc proc was rebuilt from a no-op stub and now runs end-to-end (fixed: backtick CAST identifiers, DATEDIFF(0,x) floor-to-midnight idiom, infinite freshness-wait WHILE loop in helper, MERGE multi-match dedup; reuses ExtractXMLValue UDF). However, the source daily_snapshot.etoro_Billing_Withdraw is a LIVE FULL TABLE (2007..today) whose rows are continuously re-modified — ModificationDate moves forward on each re-modification, so rows migrate between day-slices over time. Gold is a frozen daily snapshot. Re-extracting ANY day from the current live source drifts from gold (e.g., re-extracting settled day 20260615 yielded 59,416 rows vs gold's 59,835; tail day 20260620 yielded 4,827 vs 4,843). The migration table's older days matched gold ONLY because they were a verbatim one-time bulk copy (CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE AS SELECT), not because the proc can reproduce them. Honest reproduction parity is therefore not achievable in this partial-replication environment. The two days touched during testing (20260615, 20260620) were restored to their bulk state via Delta time-travel (version 29). Same environmental class as fact_snapshotequity / fact_customerunrealized_pnl.
guardrail: Parked on environmental data-availability limitation (not a code defect); no benefit to exhausting 5 passes.
